description: Simulate the dynamic response of a 2-DOF spring-mass-damper system using the ABRAVIBE toolbox in Octave. The system has masses m1=1 kg and m2=0.5 kg connected by springs with stiffnesses k1=1e4 N/m (ground to m1) and k2=5e3 N/m (m1 to m2). Use proportional (Rayleigh) damping with coefficients alpha=2 and beta=0.0002 to achieve light damping (~2%). Compute the accelerance Frequency Response Function from an input force at DOF 1 to the acceleration response at DOF 2, over a frequency range that captures both resonances of the system (they are below 50 Hz). The ABRAVIBE toolbox includes functions for computing FRFs from mass-stiffness-damping matrices and for extracting modal properties — explore what is available. Create a two-panel plot showing the FRF magnitude (in dB) and phase (in degrees) versus frequency, and mark the natural frequencies on the plot. Save the figure to /home/ga/plots/frf_2dof_system.png.
